name: Valdefuentes Trail
name_original: Sendero de Valdefuentes (SLC-VA 57) — circular desde Urueña
description: Hike the moderate 3.7-mile Sendero de Valdefuentes circular route from Urueña, offering historic views and varied terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Urueña offers trails through a landscape characterized by gentle hills, open fields, and historical sites. The region features routes that often incorporate water features like reservoirs and fountains, providing varied scenery for outdoor activities. The terrain is generally accessible, with modest elevation changes, making it suitable for a range of hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Urueña The most popular hiking route is Embalse de la Espina — circular desde el Monasterio de la Santa Espina, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete, circling a reservoir from a historic monastery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Fountains, a moderate 9.7 miles (15.6 km) path. This route explores the local area, highlighting various natural water sources and takes approximately 4 hours 5 minutes. Local hikers also love the Valdefuentes Trail, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with some elevation gain, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking around Urueña is defined by open landscapes, historical points of interest, and routes that often feature water elements like reservoirs and fountains. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Urueña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Urueña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Urueña?
A: There are over 8 hiking trails in the Urueña region, offering a variety of experiences through gentle hills, open fields, and historical sites. The routes are generally accessible, with options for both easy and moderate abilities.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Urueña offers several easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of La Santa Espina loop from La Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7-mile (7.6 km) route that takes approximately 1 hour 59 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Urueña?
A: The terrain around Urueña is characterized by gentle hills and open fields, often incorporating water features like reservoirs and fountains. The routes are generally accessible with modest elevation changes, making them suitable for a range of hiking abilities.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Urueña are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080261281/embalse-de-la-espina-circular-desde-el-monasterio-de-la-santa-espina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Espina — circular desde el Monasterio de la Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.0-mile (9.7 km) moderate trail that circles a reservoir from a historic monastery.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while hiking in Urueña?
A: Many trails pass by historical and natural points of interest. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of La Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5709148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urueña Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features water bodies like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bajoz River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Urueña suitable for families with children?
A: The generally accessible terrain and modest elevation changes make many trails suitable for families. Easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of La Santa Espina loop&lt;/a&gt;, are good choices for walks with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Urueña?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the Urueña region, especially in open, rural areas. It's always advisable to keep them on a leash and clean up after them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Urueña?
A: The best time for hiking in Urueña is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during that season.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water elements like fountains or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is known for its water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092396507/route-of-the-fountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Fountains&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights various natural water sources, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080261281/embalse-de-la-espina-circular-desde-el-monasterio-de-la-santa-espina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Espina circular route&lt;/a&gt; circles a reservoir.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Urueña?
A: The trails in Urueña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the open landscapes, historical points of interest, and the presence of water elements along the routes.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While most trails are moderate, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092396507/route-of-the-fountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Fountains&lt;/a&gt;, at 9.7 miles (15.6 km) and taking over 4 hours, offer a longer experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080232899/valdefuentes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdefuentes Trail&lt;/a&gt; also provides varied terrain with some elevation gain.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads in rural areas like Urueña can be limited. It is often recommended to use a car to access the starting points of many hiking routes in the region, though local bus services might connect to the main town.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available near the main attractions and starting points of popular trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of La Santa Espina&lt;/a&gt; or within the town of Urueña itself. Look for designated parking areas or roadside spots where permitted.
tips:
The castle of Urueña, today only preserves the outer walls, having completely disappeared the different rooms that it could have had in the past.
It was ordered to be built around the year 1060 by the Castilian monarch Fernando I the Great on the remains of an ancient Roman fortification.

The Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation is located in Urueña, in the province of Valladolid and is an outstanding example of Romanesque-Lombard art in Spain.
Built in the 12th century by order of the Infanta Sancha Raimúndez, on the site of a previous Mozarabic church. It is the only complete example of Romanesque-Lombard art in Castile and León.
The hermitage is located in a small valley a few kilometres from the town of Urueña, offering a magnificent view of the wall that surrounds the town.

faqs.question: Where does the Sendero de Valdefuentes (SLC-VA 57) trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that starts and ends in Urueña. The starting point is conveniently located next to a parking lot, making it easy to access by car.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily features accessible paths. However, hikers should be prepared for some sections that may be uneven or require sure footing, suggesting a varied landscape that could include some rockier areas.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Sendero de Valdefuentes (SLC-VA 57) route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy the natural beauty of the Montes Torozos region, often bathed in a golden light. You'll also encounter historical landmarks such as the impressive Urueña Castle and the serene Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation, offering a blend of nature and cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty, requiring good physical condition. While many paths are accessible, some sections demand sure footing. It might be suitable for active families, but beginners should be aware of its moderate rating.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de Valdefuentes (SLC-VA 57) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to respect local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Sendero de Valdefuentes (SLC-VA 57). It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be beautiful with its 'golden light,' but it's advisable to hike early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the route primarily follows the Sendero de Valdefuentes (SLC-VA 57), it also briefly intersects with sections of the Adarve and Corro de San Andrés trails.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Montes Torozos region and its proximity to the historic walled town of Urueña, the trail offers pleasant vistas of the surrounding countryside and likely views of the town itself, providing several scenic viewpoints.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like restrooms, cafes, or restaurants near the trailhead in Urueña?
faqs.answer: Urueña is a historic town, so you can expect to find amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and potentially public restrooms within the town, which is the starting and ending point of the circular route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike like this, it's advisable to bring comfortable hiking shoes suitable for varied terrain, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a charged phone. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are also recommended.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Annunciation
